reasoning: Task appears to be to produce a concise summary of the provided news article, including key names, charges, timelines, and outcomes.
output: Omar Khan, 31, who had worked for five years at The Johnson Partnership in Nottingham, was arrested. He and three others—Erlin Manahasa (no fixed address), Albert Dibra (Joyce Avenue, Nottingham), and Nazaquat Ali (Chard Street, Nottingham)—admitted the same charge of conspiracy to supply a Class A drug at Nottingham Crown Court, relating to events between 1 October and 4 December last year. They are due to be sentenced on 15 April. The court heard about the recovery of 1kg of cocaine. Digby Johnson, a partner at The Johnson firm, said Khan did not represent Khan and had set up his own office and was leaving the firm. He described Khan as a lovely, hardworking person who could be drawn into drugs and people involved in drugs. Khan appeared by video link to admit conspiracy to supply the Class A drug. Manahasa, Dibra, and Ali were remanded in custody following the hearing.
